rulings.Case Title: ROBERT LIGHTFOOT VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Subpoenas
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 28, 2010, line 1, PLAINTIFF ROBERT LIGHTFOOT'S Motion To Quash Subpoenas. OFF CALENDAR, NO PROOF OF SERVICE OR COURTESY COPIES. SEE SF LOCAL RULE 2.6B. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: BOREL PRIVATE BANK & TRUST COMPANY VS. BURT HAMROL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Burt Hamrol, Customer Service General Contracting, Inc. And Hamrol Development, Inc.'S Order To Show Cause
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 28, 2010, line 1, Defendants Burt Hamrol, Customer Service General Contracting, Inc. And Hamrol Development, Inc.'s Order To Show Cause. DENIED. HAMROL FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE A LIKELIHOOD OF PREVAILING ON THE MERITS. THE STRUCTURE OF THE LOANS WAS CLEAR ON THEIR FACE. THE DECLARATION FROM GREEN OF BOREL IS MORE CREDIBLE THAN HAMROL'S EVIDENCE. THIS CASE IS SIMILAR TO JESSEN V. KEYSTONE SAVINGS AND LOAN ASSOCIATION (1983) 142 CAL.APP.3D 454 AND MONEY DAMAGES ON THIS INVESTMENT PROPERTY WOULD MAKE THE PLAINTIFF WHOLE. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ALAN POOLE VS. DMITRY YESIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 28, 2010, line 2, CROSS-COMPLAINANT DMITRY YESIN, SVETLANA YESIN'S Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions. GRANTED. CROSS-DEFENDANT TO SUPPLY REPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES WITHOUT OBJECTIONS. CROSS-DEFENDANT TO RESPOND TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES WITH SPECIFIC REFERENCE TO PARTICULAR DOCUMENTS PROVIDE MR. POOLE'S DECLARATION THAT NO ADDITIONAL INFORMATION EXISTS. REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS DENIED. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SAPHIRE ADVISORS LLC, VS. THE SAINT JAMES EOS WINE COMPANY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 28, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT THE SAINT JAMES EOS WINE COMPANY, THE SAINT JAMES COMPANY, DALE PAISLEY, KERRY VIX DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 3RD AND 5TH CAUSES OF ACTION (FRAUD AND INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE.) PLAINTIFF TO PLEAD A VIABLE FRAUD CLAIM INCLUDING RELIANCE. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE INTERFERENCE WITH A CONTRACT BY A THIRD PARTY. DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION (BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTIES) WHICH IS PLED SUFFICIENTLY TO SURVIVE DEMURRER. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SAPHIRE ADVISORS LLC, VS. THE SAINT JAMES EOS WINE COMPANY, et al

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY VS. SEIU LOCAL 790-DONISE MANCHESTER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Denying Petitioner'S Motion To Vacate Arbitration Award And Confirming Arb Award;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 28, 2010, line 12, RESPONDENT SEIU LOCAL 790-DONISE MANCHESTER'S Motion For An Order Denying Petitioner's Motion To Vacate Arbitration Award And Confirming Arbitration Award. GRANTED. THE PROPOSED ORDER INCLUDING THE REFERENCE TO COSTS IS PROPER. IF THE PREVAILING PARTY FILES A COSTS MEMORANDUM THAT INCLUDES IMPROPER OR UNTIMELY COSTS, THEN PETITIONERS SHOULD FILE A MOTION TO TAX TO CONTEST THE COSTS. SEE CRC 8.278(c)(2) AND CRC 3.1700(b). =(302/CWW)
